'''Nathan Massey''' (born 11 July 1989) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|lf}} in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship]] in the 2000s, 2010s and 2020s.

Massey spent the majority of his playing career with the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] after progressing through the club's academy. Across a 19-year stay at Castleford, he made 302 senior appearances, ranking second only to former captain [[Michael Shenton]] in the summer era. He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup final|2014]] and [[2021 Challenge Cup final|2021]] Challenge Cup finals, the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]], and won the 2017 [[League Leaders' Shield]].

Early in his career, he spent time on loan or dual registration from Castleford at [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead Thunder]], and [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and at the [[Keighley Cougars]] and [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. He signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 2024 where he spent one season before announcing his retirement.

==Background==
Massey was born in [[Castleford]], [[West Yorkshire, England]].

Massey played junior rugby league for [[Wakefield]]-based side Walton Warriors before being signed to the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy at the age of 15.<ref>{{cite web |date=20 June 2013 |title=Tigers prop Massey signs contract extension |url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/tigers-prop-massey-signs-contract-extension-1-5782170 |accessdate=28 January 2018 |website=Pontefract and Castleford Express}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Castleford Tigers===
Massey progressed through the ranks at the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy and made his first team début on 11 March 2007 in an 88–10 victory over [[Castleford Lock Lane]] in the [[2007 Challenge Cup#Round 3|Challenge Cup]]. He was named the Tigers Academy player of the year in 2007.<ref name="2008 Contract">{{cite web |date=27 August 2008 |title=New Deal For Nathan |url=http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/news.cgi?150_16286 |publisher=Castleford Tigers RLFC |accessdate=28 January 2018 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080916233141/http://www.castigers.com/cgi-bin/news.cgi?150_16286 |archivedate=16 September 2008}}</ref>

On 24 March 2008, Massey made his [[Super League]] début for Castleford against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 March 2008 |title=Castleford 31-34 Warrington |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7300767.stm |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080327130731/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7300767.stm |archive-date=27 March 2008 |access-date=9 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In the 2008 season, Massey spent time on loan at [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and at [[York City Knights|York]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] to gain first team experience.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Martel |first=Stuart |date=2008-08-02 |title=Tigers tie-up pays dividends as York sign teenage prop |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/3566119.tigers-tie-up-pays-dividends-as-york-sign-teenage-prop/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220525201421/https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/3566119.tigers-tie-up-pays-dividends-as-york-sign-teenage-prop/ |archive-date=25 May 2022 |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref> He impressed for the Knights, who expressed a desire to extend his stay at the club into the next season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Martini |first=Peter |date=2008-08-11 |title=Knights eyeing Massey move to cap high five |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/3584946.knights-eyeing-massey-move-to-cap-high-five/ |access-date=2022-05-24 |website=The York Press}}</ref> He was given an upgraded full-time contract by Castleford in August 2008.<ref name="2008 Contract" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Martini |first=Peter |date=2008-08-28 |title=Knights extra has big future |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/3628061.knights-extra-has-big-future/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref>

Massey spent time on loan at [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead Thunder]] alongside several Castleford teammates in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-06-25 |title=Anth's a good Find – Rod |url=https://www.chroniclelive.co.uk/sport/other-sport/rugby-league/anths-good-find--rod-1456323 |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The Evening Chronicle}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Newcastle Thunder Heritage |url=https://www.thunderrugby.co.uk/club/heritage |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=Newcastle Thunder}}</ref> In March 2010, he joined [[Keighley Cougars]] on a dual registration deal.<ref>{{cite web |date=31 March 2010 |title=Castleford prop Nathan Massey joins Keighley on loan |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/8596765.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=28 January 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Murphy |first=Connor |date=2010-04-01 |title=Tigers prop adds bite to Cougars |url=https://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/6224648.tigers-prop-adds-bite-to-cougars/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=Keighley News}}</ref> Despite making an impressive debut, he suffered a severe shoulder injury against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in his first game for the club, ending his season prematurely.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Murphy |first=Connor |date=2010-04-04 |title=Cougars poised to 'turn the corner' |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/rlkeighleycougars/7984442.cougars-poised-to-turn-the-corner/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The Telegraph & Argus}}</ref><ref name="2011 York Loan">{{Cite web |last=Martini |first=Peter |date=2011-02-04 |title=York City Knights land Castleford Tigers' Nathan Massey and John Davies |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/8833772.york-city-knights-land-castleford-tigers-nathan-massey-and-john-davies/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref>

Massey began the 2011 season playing for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] through their dual registration arrangement with Castleford.<ref name="2011 York Loan" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-02-04 |title=York City Knights sign Castleford's Massey and Davies |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9387554.stm |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He had been linked with a loan move to [[Harlequins RL|Harlequins]] but the Tigers preferred he went to York, from whom he could be recalled each week if needed.<ref name="2011 York Loan" /> In his second spell with the club, he was voted the Player of the Month for February,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-03-04 |title=Nathan Massey is York City Knights' top prop |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/8890120.nathan-massey-is-york-city-knights-top-prop/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref> and scored his first competitive try on 13 March against [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Hunslet Hawks 28 - 28 York City Knights |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/matchdetails.php?tselect=10252 |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=RL Record Keepers' Club}}</ref> In June, the Knights decided against renewing Massey's temporary deal to free up salary cap space and instead develop their own players;<ref>{{Cite web |last=Martini |first=Peter |date=2011-06-04 |title=Championship debut beckons for Knights’ Adam Howard |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/9066231.championship-debut-beckons-for-knights-adam-howard/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Martini |first=Peter |date=2011-06-08 |title=Knights salary cap limit at root of Castleford loan u-turn |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/9071632.knights-salary-salary-cap-limit-at-root-of-castleford-loan-u-turn/ |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=The York Press}}</ref> he scored 2 tries against [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]] in the [[2011 Challenge Cup#Round 4|Challenge Cup]] on his return to Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-05-08 |title=Tigers find their roar |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/rochdale-vs-castleford/31791 |access-date=2022-05-25 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> Injuries to teammates afforded Massey an extended run in the Tigers' first team, and he made 15 appearances during the remainder of the season.<ref name="RLRKC" />

He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

On 30 April 2021, Massey made his 250th Castleford appearance.<ref>{{Cite web |date=14 May 2021 |title="I am proud to play for this club" – Castleford stalwart Nathan Massey signs new deal |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/i-am-proud-to-play-for-this-club-castleford-stalwart-nathan-massey-signs-new-deal |access-date=9 September 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

Massey made his 300th Castleford appearance on 8 September 2023 in the Tigers' 29–12 victory over [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 September 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 29-12 Hull FC - Cas close in on survival |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66729971 |access-date=9 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 10 November 2023, it was announced that Massey had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] for the 2024 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=10 November 2023 |title=Featherstone Rovers swoop to sign Castleford Tigers stalwart: 'I can't wait to get stuck in' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-swoop-to-sign-castleford-tigers-stalwart-i-cant-wait-to-get-stuck-in |access-date=11 November 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=10 November 2023 |title=Ex-Castleford Tigers forward Nathan Massey finds new club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-forward-nathan-massey-finds-new-club/ |access-date=10 November 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> He had originally agreed terms while Featherstone were anticipating promotion to the [[Super League]], but said he "wanted to commit to the project even though we're still in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]".<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=10 November 2023 |title=Former Castleford Tigers prop Nathan Massey follows through on Featherstone Rovers pledge despite Championship status |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-castleford-tigers-prop-nathan-massey-follows-through-on-featherstone-rovers-pledge-despite-championship-status-4405942 |access-date=11 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

===International honours===
On 4 November 2007, Massey was named as one of ten {{Rlp|in}}s for the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] in a 70–0 warm-up game defeat by [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] at [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]]'s stadium, [[Craven Park, Barrow-in-Furness|Craven Park]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 November 2007 |title=Stars earn their stripes |url=http://www.whitehavennews.co.uk/stars-earn-their-stripes-1.157628 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20141125151430/http://www.whitehavennews.co.uk/stars-earn-their-stripes-1.157628 |archive-date=25 November 2014 |access-date=30 August 2023 |website=The Whitehaven News}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=688|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120617065253/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=688|archivedate=17 June 2012 }}</ref> He has later clarified that he has "no allegiance to America", with the experience coming about through U.S. team manager Mick Robinson being football manager at Castleford.<ref name="2023 United States">{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=3 February 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers testimonial star Nathan Massey reveals bizarre story behind one-game USA career |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-testimonial-star-nathan-massey-reveals-bizarre-story-behind-one-game-usa-career-4012993 |access-date=30 August 2023 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> He has had no involvement since, being [[IRL eligibility rules|ineligible under IRL rules]].<ref name="2023 United States" />
